lenses.guidance: vv 1-12's death-narrative — Pisgah ascent, divine burial, thirty-day mourning, prophetic-incomparability verdict — closes the Pentateuch with elegy. The form is final-witness; vv 10-12's epitaph fixes Moses as the unmatched mediator.

vv 10-12's verdict — 'no prophet has risen in Israel like Moses, whom the LORD knew face to face' — sets the bar Christ alone surpasses (Heb 3:1-6, where Jesus is more than Moses; Jn 1:17, the law through Moses, grace through Jesus).

v 6's hidden-burial — 'no one knows where his grave is' — threads to Jude 9's tradition about Michael disputing over Moses's body, and to Mt 17:3's transfiguration where Moses appears alive with Christ. A canonical line on the prophet's afterlife.
